page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c1m1.large is an entry-level virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.e server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance features 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, and is configured with 2.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that this instance is positioned in the lower performance tiers. The stress-ng single-core CPU test scored 1296.39, placing it in the bottom 25%, while the multi-core CPU score of 1720.39 falls into the bottom 10%. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with a cached read-only small block bandwidth of 95.86 GB/sec and uncached large block read and write speeds of approximately 2.06 GB/sec and 2.04 GB/sec, respectively. Passmark metrics confirm these limitations, with a CPU score of 1740.41 and a database operations score of 533.35, both in the bottom 10%.

Given its shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity, this instance offers a highly cost-efficient profile for lightweight, non-critical workloads where resource density and budget are prioritized over raw performance. It is suited for basic utility tasks, simple scripting, lightweight Redis caching, or low-traffic static web serving, which benchmarked at 0.27 GB/sec. It is not suitable for memory-intensive applications, heavy database operations, or high-throughput networking.

It features 2 shared vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU resources. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1296.39 and a multi-core score of 1720.39. Memory bandwidth is also limited, recording 2.06 GB/sec for uncached large block reads. This resource profile makes the instance a budget-friendly option for lightweight utility tasks, small-scale Redis operations, and low-traffic static web serving.
